By following these guidelines, your Penguin Tetras will thrive and exhibit their lively schooling behavior and striking appearance.

Light:
Provide moderate light for 8 hours daily. Avoid low light to prevent leggy growth.

products.title: Swords
products.body_html:
Amazon Sword Care Guide

Overview: Amazon sword (Echinodorus spp.) is a popular and hardy aquatic plant commonly used in freshwater aquariums. Known for its large, broad leaves, it provides excellent cover for fish and adds a lush, green aesthetic to the tank.

Lighting:

  • Moderate to High: Amazon swords thrive under moderate to high lighting. Insufficient light can lead to slower growth and smaller leaves.
  • Duration: Aim for 10-12 hours of light per day.

Water Conditions:

  • Temperature: 72-82°F (22-28°C).
  • pH: 6.5-7.5. It prefers slightly acidic to neutral water.
  • Hardness: Soft to moderately hard water (3-8 dGH).

Substrate:

  • Nutrient-rich: Amazon swords have extensive root systems and benefit from a nutrient-rich substrate like aquarium soil or planted tank substrate.
  • Root Tabs: Supplementing with root tabs every few months can help provide essential nutrients.

Planting and Placement:

  • Depth: Plant the Amazon sword deeply enough to cover the roots, but keep the crown (where the leaves meet the roots) above the substrate to prevent rot.
  • Placement: Place in the midground or background of the aquarium due to its size.

Maintenance:

  • Trimming: Remove yellowing or damaged leaves regularly to promote new growth and maintain the plant's appearance.
  • Fertilization: Regular fertilization with liquid fertilizers and root tabs can help support robust growth. Amazon swords are heavy feeders.

Water Changes:

  • Perform regular water changes (20-30% weekly) to maintain water quality and support plant health.

Propagation:

  • Amazon swords propagate through runners and side shoots. Separate the new plantlets from the mother plant once they develop a few leaves and roots, then plant them in the substrate.

Common Issues:

  • Nutrient Deficiencies: Yellowing leaves may indicate a lack of nutrients, especially iron. Regular fertilization and root tabs can prevent this.
  • Algae: Maintain a balance of nutrients and light to prevent algae overgrowth.

By providing the right conditions and care, Amazon swords will thrive and become a centerpiece in your aquarium, offering both beauty and functionality as a natural habitat for your fish.

products.title: Petrified Wood
products.body_html:
1. Select the Right Stones

  • Quantity and Size: Choose the number and size of stones based on your aquarium’s size and the aquascape design you envision.
  • Aquascaping Plan: Plan your layout. Sketching your design can help visualize how the stones will be arranged.

2. Clean the Stones

  • Rinse: Thoroughly rinse the stones under running water to remove any dust or loose particles.
  • Scrub: Use a brush (not used for any chemicals) to scrub off any dirt or debris. Do not use soap or detergents.

3. Test for Alkalinity

  • Vinegar Test: Drip vinegar on the stone. If it fizzes, it indicates the stone may affect your water's pH. Seiryu stones can slightly raise the pH and hardness, so consider this if you have fish sensitive to pH changes.

4. Prepare the Aquarium

  • Substrate: Add the substrate to your tank before placing the stones. This helps anchor the stones and provides a natural look.
  • Water Level: Partially fill the tank with water to make it easier to position the stones.

5. Position the Stones

  • Foundation Stones: Place the largest stones first as they will serve as the main features of your aquascape.
  • Stability: Ensure the stones are stable and not likely to topple over. If needed, bury the base of the stones slightly in the substrate for extra stability.
  • Secondary Stones: Arrange smaller stones around the main stones to create a cohesive design.

6. Final Adjustments

  • Fill the Tank: Once the stones are securely in place, slowly fill the tank with water to the desired level.
  • Check Water Parameters: Monitor pH and hardness levels over the next few days to ensure they remain within acceptable ranges for your fish.

7. Acclimate Fish and Plants

  • Introduce Plants: Plant any live plants around the stones, considering their light and nutrient requirements.
  • Add Fish: Gradually introduce fish to the tank, ensuring they are acclimated to any potential changes in water chemistry.

Larger rocks may require a minimum weight. Please contact us for assistance on various rock sizes before placing your order.

  • Aquascaping Plan: Plan your layout. Sketching your design can help visualize how the stones will be arranged.

2. Clean the Stones

  • Rinse: Thoroughly rinse the stones under running water to remove any dust or loose particles.
  • Scrub: Use a brush (not used for any chemicals) to scrub off any dirt or debris. Do not use soap or detergents.

3. Test for Alkalinity

  • Vinegar Test: Drip vinegar on the stone. If it fizzes, it indicates the stone may affect your water's pH. Seiryu stones can slightly raise the pH and hardness, so consider this if you have fish sensitive to pH changes.

4. Prepare the Aquarium

  • Substrate: Add the substrate to your tank before placing the stones. This helps anchor the stones and provides a natural look.
  • Water Level: Partially fill the tank with water to make it easier to position the stones.

5. Position the Stones

  • Foundation Stones: Place the largest stones first as they will serve as the main features of your aquascape.
  • Stability: Ensure the stones are stable and not likely to topple over. If needed, bury the base of the stones slightly in the substrate for extra stability.
  • Secondary Stones: Arrange smaller stones around the main stones to create a cohesive design.

6. Final Adjustments

  • Fill the Tank: Once the stones are securely in place, slowly fill the tank with water to the desired level.
  • Check Water Parameters: Monitor pH and hardness levels over the next few days to ensure they remain within acceptable ranges for your fish.

7. Acclimate Fish and Plants

  • Introduce Plants: Plant any live plants around the stones, considering their light and nutrient requirements.
  • Add Fish: Gradually introduce fish to the tank, ensuring they are acclimated to any potential changes in water chemistry.

Larger rocks may require a minimum weight. Please contact us for assistance on various rock sizes before placing your order.

products.title: Serpae Tetra
products.body_html:
Serpae Tetra – Vibrant and Active Schooling Fish

The Serpae Tetra (Hyphessobrycon eques) is a popular freshwater fish known for its bright red-orange body and black-edged fins, adding vivid color and dynamic movement to community aquariums. These active schooling fish thrive in groups of six or more, creating a striking visual display in planted or community tanks.

Serpae Tetras are hardy and easy to care for, making them ideal for both beginner and experienced aquarists. They prefer well-maintained aquariums with stable water parameters and gentle water flow. While generally peaceful, they may exhibit mild fin-nipping, so it’s best to keep them with fast-moving or robust tankmates.

Care Requirements:

  • Temperature: 72–79°F

  • pH: 6.0–7.5

  • Temperament: Semi-aggressive, schooling

  • Tank Size: Minimum 20 gallons

  • Diet: Flake food, micro pellets, frozen or live foods

Highlights:

  • Bright red-orange body with black-edged fins

  • Active, schooling fish for dynamic aquariums

  • Hardy and beginner-friendly

  • Ideal for planted and community freshwater tanks

Buy Serpae Tetras for sale online to add vibrant color and lively movement to your aquarium.

products.title: Christmas Moss on Coconut Bridge
products.body_html:
Christmas Moss Care Guide

Overview: Christmas moss (Vesicularia montagnei) is a popular aquatic plant for aquariums, known for its unique, festive appearance. It is relatively easy to care for and can thrive in a variety of conditions, making it an excellent choice for both beginner and experienced aquarists.

Lighting:

  • Moderate to High: Christmas moss prefers moderate to high lighting. Too little light can slow its growth, while too much light may lead to algae problems.
  • Duration: Aim for 8-10 hours of light per day.

Water Conditions:

  • Temperature: 64-82°F (18-28°C). Optimal growth occurs between 68-77°F (20-25°C).
  • pH: 5.0-7.5. It can tolerate a range of pH levels but prefers slightly acidic to neutral water.
  • Hardness: Soft to moderately hard water (2-15 dGH).

Planting and Placement:

  • Attachment: Christmas moss can be attached to rocks, driftwood, or other aquarium decorations using fishing line, cotton thread, or super glue.
  • Substrate: It does not require substrate as it absorbs nutrients from the water column.

Maintenance:

  • Trimming: Regular trimming helps maintain its shape and encourages healthy growth. Use sharp scissors to trim excess growth.
  • Cleaning: Gently clean to remove any debris or algae that may accumulate on the moss.
  • Fertilization: While not strictly necessary, occasional liquid fertilizers can promote lush growth. Use a balanced aquarium fertilizer.

Water Changes:

  • Perform regular water changes (20-30% weekly) to keep water conditions stable and reduce the risk of algae growth.

Propagation:

  • Christmas moss can be easily propagated by dividing the moss into smaller portions and attaching them to different areas or objects in the aquarium.

Common Issues:

  • Algae: Ensure good water circulation and maintain a balance of nutrients to prevent algae overgrowth.
  • Debris Accumulation: Avoid excessive debris buildup by keeping the aquarium clean and performing regular maintenance.

By following these guidelines, your Christmas moss will thrive, adding a lush, festive touch to your aquarium.

products.title: Red Cherry Shrimp
products.body_html:
Red Cherry Shrimp – Live Freshwater Shrimp for Planted Tanks, Nano Aquariums & Algae Control

Product Description:
Add vibrant color and natural cleaning to your aquarium with Red Cherry Shrimp (Neocaridina davidi). These hardy freshwater shrimp are a favorite among aquarists for their deep red coloration, peaceful nature, and algae-eating abilities. Perfect for planted tanks, nano aquariums, and peaceful community setups, they bring life and movement to your aquascape while helping maintain water quality.

Red Cherry Shrimp constantly graze on algae, biofilm, and detritus, keeping your tank cleaner with minimal effort. They are adaptable and beginner-friendly, making them ideal for first-time shrimp keepers as well as experienced breeders. With proper care, they breed readily, creating a self-sustaining colony over time.

Care Guidelines:

  • Water Parameters: pH 6.5–8.0 | Temperature 65–78°F | TDS 150–250 ppm

  • Ammonia & Nitrite: 0 ppm (toxic to shrimp, must always remain at zero)

  • Nitrate: Below 20 ppm (higher levels can cause stress and reduced breeding)

  • Diet: Algae, biofilm, shrimp pellets, blanched vegetables

  • Minimum Tank Size: 5 gallons

  • Compatibility: Best with small peaceful fish, snails, and other dwarf shrimp

Why Choose Our Red Cherry Shrimp:

  • Brilliant red coloration for maximum visual impact

  • Natural algae control and tank maintenance

  • Hardy and easy to care for, ideal for beginners

  • Live arrival guarantee with healthy, tank-raised shrimp

Each shrimp is hand-selected for health and vibrant color before shipping in insulated, oxygen-packed containers to ensure safe arrival. Whether you’re starting your first shrimp tank or expanding your breeding colony, Red Cherry Shrimp are a stunning and functional addition to any freshwater aquarium.
